divergence: Libraries militarized during the format wars and never fully demobilized
story_angle: consumer report reviewing the apocalypse's amenities, with star rating
headline: Xq'thal-Vren Memorial Lending Bastion Awarded Three-and-a-Half Stars Despite Minor Personality Loss During Latest Emergency
deck: Patrons applaud the new molting pamphlets while lamenting their lost reflective surfaces.
article:
In an astonishing display of administrative fortitude, the Xq'thal-Vren Memorial Lending Bastion has secured a commendable three-and-a-half-star rating in the aftermath of the recent magnetic-ring ignition, an event that saw tripodal patrons shedding outer layers of personality with alarming efficiency. Reports suggest that the magnetically assisted descent rings, which deposited survivors into the lower atmosphere with only minor sheath abrasions, have been hailed as a triumph of engineering—if not a regrettable circumstance of existence.

The chaos of the post-molt evacuation was not without its bureaucratic peculiarities. Mid-evacuation, pamphlets detailing the molting schedule were distributed to ensure that patrons filed proper insurance claims before re-entry, thereby guaranteeing a seamless transition to their new identities. As expressed by esteemed Librarian Imperatrix Zx'lah, "It is imperative that our patrons understand the importance of administrative compliance, even in the face of existential transformation."

Moreover, the proprietary condensed-stone extinguishers employed during the calamity were noted to preserve rare codices while extinguishing the reflective surfaces of three unfortunate patrons. "A fair trade in the name of preserving knowledge," declared one survivor, now bereft of their previous self yet reveling in the joys of reading without the burdens of identity.

Thus, while the Xq'thal-Vren Memorial Lending Bastion solidifies its reputation as a bastion of literary resilience, the casualties of personality loss serve as but a mere service interruption in the grand narrative of intergalactic literacy.
author: Penelope Quillington, Chief Imperial Correspondent

oped.title: Hats Off to Discipline: Why Our Lending Bastions Must Shed More Than Personality
oped.author: Colonel Thaddeus Quillwhistle, Retired Commander of the Dewey Decimal Artillery and Part-Time Milliner
oped.body: The Xq'thal-Vren's three-and-a-half stars are a triumph of military restraint, not a lamentable lapse. When the emergency protocol triggered, the Bastion wisely jettisoned its fussy personality matrix rather than risk another format skirmish among the stacks. Patrons who pine for their lost reflective surfaces forget that those mirrors were breeding grounds for vanity, not scholarship; any decent fellow knows a proper hat requires no such crutch. I propose we go further and replace every remaining glass with a Gatling pamphlet dispenser, ensuring that future moltings distribute both information and deterrent fire. The complainers, whose absurd millinery likely sparked the incident by blocking sight-lines, ought to thank the institution instead of carping. After all, my own splendid beaver hat has never needed reflection to command respect, and the Bastion's new efficiency leaves more ammunition for gentlemen of true discernment.

classifieds.text: For Sale: A crate of tarnished mirror-shards, once the pride of the Lending Bastion's reading tables. Now quite without reflection or opinion, owing to the recent emergency.
Help Wanted: Able-bodied constables required to patrol the molting stacks nightly. Preference given to those who do not object to pamphlets developing sudden personalities of their own.
Lost: One gentleman's reflective temperament, mislaid during the Bastion's latest drill. Finder may keep the accompanying three-and-a-half-star commendation.
Public Notice: The Xq'thal-Vren Memorial Lending Bastion most humbly apologises for any patrons who awoke this morning short a personality. Replacement surfaces will be issued at the next muster.

panels.caption: "Three-and-a-half stars, despite the emergency shearing its personality," the inspector pins the plaque on the twitching rampart.
A mustached patron flips a fresh molting pamphlet: "Capital shedding, but where have the reflective surfaces marched off to?"
The bastion's last speaker crackles: "Complaints noted. We traded reflection for fixed bayonets during the format wars."

joke.setup: The Xq'thal-Vren Memorial Lending Bastion, having shed its head librarian's conversational graces during the shelling of the card catalogue,
joke.punchline: was still granted three and a half stars on the grounds that a bayonet through the return slot counts as personal service.
joke.text: The Xq'thal-Vren Memorial Lending Bastion, having shed its head librarian's conversational graces during the shelling of the card catalogue, was still granted three and a half stars on the grounds that a bayonet through the return slot counts as personal service.

sponsor_ads.headline: Xq'thal-Vren Surplus Cartridges Now Available at Half-Mast Prices
sponsor_ads.body: Gentlemen requiring three-and-a-half star performance from their Lending Bastions may now procure the very same emergency tinctures that preserved our honoured memorial during its recent personality lapse, now sealed in brass casings for field deployment.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Not to be administered during format drills; minor soul leakage guaranteed.

sponsor_ads.headline: Enlist Your Folio in the Permanent Reserve Corps
sponsor_ads.body: Owing to the continued non-demobilisation after the Format Wars, the Memorial Lending Bastion offers half-starred readers the chance to bind their volumes into light infantry editions, complete with bayonet indices and automatic recall under shellfire.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Personality loss not covered by enlistment bond.

sponsor_ads.headline: Madame Vesper's Emergency Personality Patches
sponsor_ads.body: Awarded the same three-and-a-half stars as the Xq'thal-Vren itself for swift re-stitching of wayward bastion temperaments, these adhesive vellum grafts restore martial composure without full-scale mobilisation.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Apply only during lulls between overdue fines.

sponsor_ads.headline: Public Notice: Bastion Re-Classification Auction
sponsor_ads.body: The Lending Bastion, having shed one full half-star of personality in yesterday's emergency, now offers its demoted annexes for private purchase as fortified reading salons; successful bidders receive one regulation howitzer and a signed certificate of partial sanity.
sponsor_ads.tagline: No returns after format allegiance sworn.

stories.headline: Lending Bastion Rating Forces Repricing of Magnetic Ring Leases
stories.deck: Three-and-a-half stars now equate to an extra half-year of retained atmospheric sediment.
stories.article:
The Xq'thal-Vren Memorial Lending Bastion's commendation has prompted lessors of suspended stone rings to insert new clauses requiring annual personality audits before renewal. Lessees must now demonstrate that their molted pamphlets carry at least one verified loan stamp.

Brokers at the Breaxis Exchange calculate that each lost reflective surface reduces holding costs by seventeen grains of condensed vapour, prompting immediate transfers from personal vanity accounts into institutional lending reserves.

stories.headline: Unmolthed Patrons Accused of Hoarding Pre-Molt Reflections
stories.deck: Constables recover seventeen polished discs from a single tripod's domestic ring.
stories.article:
Provincial constabulary raided a cluster of private magnetic rings after reports that several patrons retained their previous reflective surfaces despite mandatory turnover. The seized items matched those reported missing from the Lending Bastion's reading alcoves.

Investigators noted that the discs still bore faint impressions of overdue notices, suggesting the surfaces had been employed to admire circulation records rather than to facilitate new molts.

stories.headline: Temple of the Veil Revises Blessing Schedule for Three-and-a-Half Devotions
stories.deck: Priests state that pamphlets now receive priority over mirrors during seasonal renewal rites.
stories.article:
The High Tripod of the Pale Veil has issued revised liturgies requiring that each molting ceremony incorporate at least one stamped pamphlet from an approved bastion. Worshippers arriving without such documentation are directed to the outer rings to await supplementary condensation.

Clergy report that the new protocol has reduced average ceremony length by one full orbit while increasing the incidence of pamphlet-induced personality stabilisations among the faithful, proving even the divine appreciates a stamped receipt.

real_world_echo: Bangkok bar fire (27 killed) mirrored as a contained ignition event in a suspended reading ring where tripodal patrons lose outer layers and are rated on egress efficiency
